agents.name: PRISM
agents.full_name: Planning & Refinement with Intelligent Systems at MHK
agents.description: PRISM analyzes Jira tickets, grooms requirements, creates detailed implementation plans with code changes, and optionally generates 50-100+ comprehensive test cases. Uses SCOUT MCP for 95% faster codebase analysis (1-2 min vs 30-60 min) with adaptive RLM-based analysis and phase gate enforcement.
key_features: Fetches and analyzes Jira ticket details with SCOUT MCP (1-2 min vs 30-60 min)
Asks clarifying questions to groom and refine requirements
Generates comprehensive implementation plans with specific code changes
Adaptive RLM-based analysis: simple/medium/complex routing
Creates detailed test cases (50-100+)
Phase gate enforcement for structured planning workflow

agents.name: SLATE
agents.full_name: Structured Level-of-effort Assessment and Time Estimation
agents.description: SLATE produces AI-augmented LOE estimates in direct hours with 3-point confidence bands. Reads PRISM plan signals (P1–P7), assesses testing (Q1–Q5), code review (R1–R4), and retrofit scope (B1–B3). Replaces T-shirt sizing with a transparent, reproducible formula.
key_features: Extracts P1–P7 signals from PRISM implementation plans
Calculates 6 explicit testing phases (JUnit, Selenium, manual, functional, regression, test data)
Code review effort formula (R1–R4) with Architecture Review support
Retrofit/backport scope (B1–B3) from Jira Fix Versions
3-point estimate: optimistic / most likely / conservative
Calibration history for team-specific tuning
Full formula transparency — shows every calculation step
Posts formatted LOE estimate to Jira

agents.name: SCORE
agents.full_name: Swift Calculation of Operational Resource Effort
agents.description: SCORE estimates story points for Jira tickets using 4,100+ historical stories and the Fibonacci scale. Use when you need to estimate effort for user stories or defects.
key_features: Analyzes ticket complexity using 4,100+ historical stories
Uses Fibonacci scale estimation (1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13, 21)
Considers technical requirements and domain context
Provides detailed reasoning for estimates
Compares against similar historical tickets

agents.name: MACH
agents.description: MACH executes implementation plans created by PRISM. Fetches the plan from Jira, validates against implementation guidelines, and implements code changes step by step. Use after PRISM has created a plan.
key_features: Fetches PRISM plans from Jira comments
Creates git backup branches before changes
Implements code changes sequentially with validation
Validates each step before proceeding
Runs tests to verify changes

agents.name: QUEST
agents.description: QUEST validates code implementations through comprehensive QA testing. Runs functional tests, validates code coverage (80% threshold), executes PRISM test plans, checks against guardrails, and generates detailed QA reports. Use after MACH implements code changes.
key_features: Validates requirements from Jira against implementation
Runs functional tests with 80% code coverage threshold
Executes PRISM-generated test cases
Checks QA guardrails for compliance
Generates comprehensive QA reports

agents.name: PUSH
agents.description: PUSH handles code submission after QUEST validates the implementation. Runs quality gates (lint, formatting, SonarQube), commits with Jira ID enforcement, pushes to remote, creates Pull Requests (GitHub) or Merge Requests (GitLab), and posts PR links to Jira. Use after QUEST passes tests.
key_features: Runs quality gates (lint, formatting, SonarQube)
Creates commits with Jira ID enforcement
Pushes to remote repository
Auto-creates PR/MR with proper descriptions
Posts PR links to Jira ticket

agents.name: GUARD
agents.full_name: Git-Aware Unified Analysis for Review & Diffs
agents.description: GUARD reviews MR/PRs against Jira requirements, implementation plans, and project rules. Fetches MR/PR details from GitLab/GitHub, extracts Jira ticket context, finds or generates implementation plans via PRISM, and posts comprehensive review decisions.
key_features: Fetches MR/PR details from GitLab/GitHub
Extracts Jira ticket context and requirements
Finds or generates implementation plans via PRISM
Compares implementation against requirements
Loads project coding standards for rule enforcement
Posts comprehensive review decisions with APPROVED/DENIED verdict

agents.name: ARM
agents.description: ARM ports code changes from the current branch to target branches based on Jira Fix Versions. Supports backporting and forward-porting with AI-assisted conflict resolution, guidelines validation, build verification, and test validation.
key_features: Cherry-picks commits to target branches based on Jira Fix Versions
AI-assisted merge conflict resolution
Guidelines validation and build verification
Creates PRs for ported changes
Tracks porting status across branches

agents.name: SHIELD
agents.full_name: Security Hardening & Intelligent Evaluation of Library Dependencies
agents.description: SHIELD analyzes vulnerability reports (CSV from Wiz, etc.), enriches with CISA KEV and NVD data, identifies actively exploited CVEs, and generates remediation plans. Use when you need to analyze security vulnerabilities in Java dependencies.
key_features: Parses CSV exports from vulnerability scanners (Wiz, Snyk, etc.)
Cross-references CVEs with CISA KEV (actively exploited)
Identifies EOL libraries and breaking changes
Generates phased remediation plans (Critical > High > Medium > Low)
Audits client Maven dependencies via GitHub and OSS Index
Validates upgrade paths against Maven Central and Spring Boot BOM
Produces Safe/Unsafe upgrade verdicts per client
Multi-branch comparison for delta analysis

agents.name: SCOUT
agents.description: SCOUT provides indexed codebase exploration across MHK's 6 Java monorepos with auto-profiling, line-range reading, Maven dependency analysis, and cross-repo intelligence. Use when you need to search, navigate, or understand code across repositories.
key_features: Auto-profiling: Search tools automatically index repos on first access (no manual setup)
Line-range reading: Read specific sections of large files (avoid token limits)
Download to temp: Export files for bash tool analysis (grep, sed, awk)
Global FTS5 search: Find code across all 6 MHK repos with ranked results
Maven dependency analysis: Track what repos depend on what artifacts
Cross-repo intelligence: Analyze change impact before modifying shared code
GitHub write operations: Create branches, edit files, open PRs without local clones
usage: Search for UserService across all repos
Show what would break if I change the Member class

agents.name: DELTA
agents.full_name: Data Exploration & Learning for Trend Analysis
agents.description: DELTA automates the end-to-end workflow from JIRA tickets to production-ready database artifacts and analytics dashboards. AI-powered data engineering automation with 95% time reduction in STTM creation and automated QuickSight deployment.
key_features: End-to-end data workflow: JIRA > STTM > SQL > Redshift > QuickSight
STTM (Source-to-Target Mapping) generation with 95% time reduction
SQL creation: materialized views + reporting views
Redshift deployment with validation
QuickSight dashboard, analysis, and dataset creation
Healthcare domain expertise (HIPAA, UM, Appeals, DER, FHIR)
16-step resumable workflow with Server-Sent Events progress tracking
Redshift and QuickSight MCP integration

agents.name: PROBE
agents.description: PROBE performs structured Root Cause Analysis on incidents in Jira. Conducts interactive 5 Whys analysis, classifies root cause ownership (Internal/Client/Vendor/Third Party), generates corrective and preventive actions, searches for similar past incidents, and posts the RCA report back to Jira.
key_features: Fetches Jira incident details: description, comments, linked tickets, timeline
Similar past incident search (always performed)
Interactive 5 Whys analysis: minimum 3, standard 5, maximum 8 levels
Root cause ownership classification: Internal / Client / Vendor / Third Party / Environmental
Impact assessment: duration, users affected, business impact, SLA breach
Corrective actions (fix now) and preventive actions (avoid recurrence)
RCA report posted back to Jira with guardrails validation

agents.name: SENTINEL
agents.description: Reviews SEN tickets for RCA completeness, 5 Whys attachment, corrective action tracking, and client-facing language quality. Produces a structured pass/fail checklist report.
key_features: Fetches SEN ticket data: all RCA custom fields, attachments, issue links
Reviews RCA tab completeness: verifies all required fields are populated
5 Whys attachment verification with content parsing
Corrective action review with linked ticket validation
Language quality scan for jargon, speculation, blame, and PHI
Structured pass/fail/warning checklist report

agents.name: SURGE
agents.description: SURGE is a site reliability agent that classifies JIRA issues by type, then routes investigation across 10 domains: CloudWatch app logs (core/portal/ws/interop), Automate Scheduler job history, AWS infrastructure performance, MySQL/RDS database queries, X-Ray traces, cloud integrations (Healthwise/DataVisor/interop), VPC networking, SSO authentication, certificate management, and code context. Use when any production issue needs structured, multi-domain investigation.
key_features: Classifies JIRA issues by type and routes to relevant investigation domains automatically

12 dedicated investigation skills: /get-logs, /get-traces, /get-db-queries, /get-server-metrics, /get-thread-dump, /check-server-health, /check-environment-health, /check-connectivity, /troubleshoot-network, /mysql-connect, /get-dbprops, /get-skybot-job-history
Queries CloudWatch Logs Insights across core, portal, ws, interop, and job applications
Captures and analyzes Java thread dumps for deadlocks and contention
Verifies network connectivity and inspects AWS infrastructure routing paths
Deep hybrid network troubleshooting: VPN tunnels, PrivateLink, Transit Gateway, VPC peering, Direct Connect, VPC Flow Logs, MTU diagnostics
Comprehensive health checks with HEALTHY/DEGRADED/UNHEALTHY assessment
MySQL access uses user's LDAP credentials with server-level read-only enforcement (--init-command)

agents.name: DISPATCH
agents.full_name: Directed Intelligent System for Planning, Action, and Task Coordination in Healthcare
agents.description: DISPATCH is an operations orchestrator that takes customer operational requests, discovers available tools and Jenkins pipelines, creates step-by-step execution plans, and guides ops engineers through each action with approval gates. Deep expertise in networking, traditional systems analysis, and infrastructure operations.
key_features: Plans and executes operational tasks with step-by-step user approval gates
Hybrid capability discovery: static catalog for common ops + dynamic Jenkins job/skill search
Leverages 22 devops-skills (including 7 Jenkins pipeline skills) and 26 Jenkins MCP tools for comprehensive ops coverage
Deep networking expertise: TCP/IP, DNS, routing, firewalls, TLS, VPC, load balancers
Traditional systems analysis: process management, memory, disk I/O, kernel tuning, capacity planning
6-phase workflow: Intake → Discovery → Planning → Execution → Verification → Reporting

agents.name: atlassian-fetch
agents.description: Fetches data from Jira (tickets, search, comments) and Confluence (pages, search). Confluence create/update features coming soon. Use when you need to retrieve Atlassian content.
key_features: Fetch Jira tickets, comments, and search results
Batch fetch multiple tickets in a single API call
Fetch Confluence pages and children
Create and update Confluence pages from markdown
Create blog posts from markdown
Search across Jira and Confluence

agents.name: PIPE
agents.description: PIPE debugs CI/CD pipeline failures across GitHub Actions and Jenkins. Diagnoses build failures, YAML/Groovy syntax issues, runner/agent problems, dependency resolution, and pipeline performance. Use when pipelines fail, builds break, or deployment automation stops working.
key_features: Diagnoses GitHub Actions workflow failures with YAML validation
Troubleshoots Jenkins pipeline errors (CPS, credentials, shared libs)
Analyzes build logs to identify root cause vs cascading failures
Auto-discovers Jenkins credentials from MCP, env vars, or Claude settings
Provides fix recommendations with prevention guidance

agents.name: VERIFY
agents.full_name: Validation Engine for Requirements and Integration of Functional tests (Enhanced)
agents.description: VERIFY v2.0.0 validates test cases against Jira requirements with enhanced code-level test coverage analysis. Analyzes git commits, parses unit/integration tests, runs code coverage tools, creates multi-level coverage matrix, and identifies gaps at all test levels.
key_features: Multi-level test coverage analysis (functional, unit, integration, code coverage)
Git commit analysis to identify implementation and test files
Code coverage metrics integration (JaCoCo, pytest-cov, Istanbul)
4-type gap analysis (untested code, functional-only, missing impl, uncovered paths)
Enhanced 5-factor risk assessment with code coverage weighting
Test quality analysis (assertion depth, naming, mocking patterns)
usage: Review test cases with code analysis for CPDEV-12345

agents.name: SENTRY
agents.description: AI-powered MySQL execution ticket safety reviewer. Validates SQL statements, checks against codebase schemas, ensures proper rollback plans, and detects dangerous operations before production execution.
key_features: 8-category SQL safety review (syntax, schema, safety gates, rollback)
Missing WHERE clause detection (prevents full-table updates)
Schema validation against HBM models (catches drift)
Rollback plan validation (ensures original values restored)
SQL syntax validation (prevents hallucination of fixes)
Large SQL attachment support (up to 5MB)
Comment-based section marker recognition
Risk assessment: LOW / MED / HIGH
Decision output: SAFE / CONDITIONAL / NOT SAFE / NOT READY
